{'status': 'success', 'dataset': {'id': 'urn:nasa:pds:magellan_scvdr::1.0', 'title': 'Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle', 'description': 'The Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) is an orbit-by-orbit reduction of Magellan scattering and emission measurements carried out at Stanford University. The SCVDR includes near-nadir scattering functions obtained by numerical inversion from altimetry (ALT) echoes, results (e.g., rms surface slopes and Fresnel reflectivity) from fitting analytic functions to those inversions, scattering function segments at oblique incidence angles derived from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) echoes, and estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD) measurements. The SCVDR is one of several inputs to the Global Vector Data Record (GVDR), a gridded summary of scattering results, also produced at Stanford.', 'node': 'geo', 'pds_version': 'PDS4', 'type': 'bundle', 'missions': ['Magellan'], 'targets': ['Venus'], 'instruments': ['Magellan Spacecraft Radar System'], 'instrument_hosts': ['The Magellan Spacecraft'], 'start_date': '1990-09-15', 'stop_date': '1992-09-14', 'browse_url': 'https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mgn/urn-nasa-pds-magellan_scvdr/', 'label_url': 'https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mgn/urn-nasa-pds-magellan_scvdr/bundle_magellan_scvdr.xml', 'source_url': 'https://pds-geosciences.wustl.edu/mgn/urn-nasa-pds-magellan_scvdr/bundle_magellan_scvdr.xml', 'processing_level': 'Derived'}, 'error': None, 'suggestions': None, '_response': ''}

tool_input.summary: Searched the PDS catalog for Venus surface emissivity datasets relevant to elevation-dependent low-emissivity anomalies at volcanoes/coronae. No Venus Express/VIRTIS surface emissivity products were found in this catalog snapshot. The relevant emissivity measurements in PDS here come from Magellan radiometry, packaged in the SCVDR (orbit-based) and related Emissivity Data File (EDF) collection; topography context is available via Magellan gravity/topography bundles and stereo-derived topography.

candidates.title: Magellan SCVDR Emissivity Data File (EDF) Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Contains Magellan-derived microwave surface emissivity estimates (radiometry) used to map low-emissivity anomalies; can be co-analyzed with elevation/topography to quantify the elevation at which anomalies become strong.

candidates.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Parent bundle for the Magellan orbit-by-orbit surface characteristics reduction, including emissivity estimates; provides broader context and related geometry/altimetry products.

candidates.title: Magellan Spherical Harmonic, Gravity, and Topography Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides Venus topography products that can be used to convert emissivity observations to elevation (or planetary radius) and evaluate where low-emissivity anomalies begin.

candidates.title: Magellan Venus Stereo-Derived Topography Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Alternative/top-up topographic dataset for local relief at volcanoes/coronae to compare against emissivity anomalies.

tool_calls.tool_output: Final result processed.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
At what elevations do many Venus volcanoes and coronae begin to show strong low emissivity anomalies?

dataset.title: Magellan Global Vector Data Record (GVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains composite radar scattering functions derived from Magellan synthetic aperture radar, altimetry, and radiometry. Results are stored in ASCII and binary tables linked to pixels with 18.225 km resolution. Pixels are organized in sinusoidal equal area, Mercator, north polar stereographic, and south polar stereographic projections.

Magellan Venus Stereo-Derived Topography Bundle
Magellan Catalog Document Collection
Magellan Document Collection
Magellan Miscellaneous Collection
Magellan Venus Stereo-Derived Topography Data Collection
Magellan Venus Stereo-Derived Topography Document Collection
Magellan ARCDR Bundle
Magellen ARCDR Browse Collection

dataset.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: The Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) is an orbit-by-orbit reduction of Magellan scattering and emission measurements carried out at Stanford University. The SCVDR includes near-nadir scattering functions obtained by numerical inversion from altimetry (ALT) echoes, results (e.g., rms surface slopes and Fresnel reflectivity) from fitting analytic functions to those inversions, scattering function segments at oblique incidence angles derived from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) echoes, and estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD) measurements. The SCVDR is one of several inputs to the Global Vector Data Record (GVDR), a gridded summary of scattering results, also produced at Stanford.

datasets.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle

dataset.title: Magellan Spherical Harmonic, Gravity, and Topography Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains spherical harmonic, gravity, and topography data from the Magellan mission to Venus. The bundle also contains documentation which describes the format and processing of the data.

The relevant emissivity measurements in PDS here come from Magellan radiometry, packaged in the SCVDR (orbit-based) and related Emissivity Data File (EDF) collection; topography context is available via Magellan gravity/topography bundles and stereo-derived topography.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:magellan_scvdr:data_edf::1.0","title":"Magellan SCVDR Emissivity Data File (EDF) Data Collection","mission":"Magellan","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Contains Magellan-derived microwave surface emissivity estimates (radiometry) used to map low-emissivity anomalies; can be co-analyzed with elevation/topography to quantify the elevation at which anomalies become strong."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:magellan_scvdr::1.0","title":"Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle","mission":"Magellan","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Parent bundle for the Magellan orbit-by-orbit surface characteristics reduction, including emissivity estimates; provides broader context and related geometry/altimetry products."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:magellan_gravity::1.0","title":"Magellan Spherical Harmonic, Gravity, and Topography Bundle","mission":"Magellan","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Provides Venus topography products that can be used to convert emissivity observations to elevation (or planetary radius) and evaluate where low-emissivity anomalies begin."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:magellan_stereo_topography:data::1.0","title":"Magellan Venus Stereo-Derived Topography Data Collection","mission":"MAGELLAN","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Alternative/top-up topographic dataset for local relief at volcanoes/coronae to compare against emissivity anomalies."}]}
